points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf3$V1)),log(dfDHf3$Z),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)
points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf3_LLL$V1)),log(dfDHf3_LLL$Z),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)


plot(log(as.numeric(df$V2)),log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=c(log(1e0),log(1e25)),ylim=log(c(0.5,100000)),
     main="growth of largest DHf3 |Z| peaks \n log(|Z|*7/7.2077509) vs log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)) plot",xlab="log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)) value",ylab="log(|Z|*7/7.2077509) value");grid(col="gray2")
points(log(as.numeric(df2$V2)),log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(dft$t)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points(log(sqrt(dfr$t/2/pi)),log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(dgr$V1)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(as.numeric(dhr$Q1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)


points(log(as.numeric(di$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(di$V2)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(di$V2)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf3$V2)),log(dfDHf3$Z*7/7.2077509),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)
points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf3_LLL$V2)),log(dfDHf3_LLL$Z*7/7.2077509),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)

lines(seq(.1,50,by=.1),1.274014+0.866363*(seq(.1,50,by=.1)^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col="brown",lty=6)

plot((log(as.numeric(df$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=log(c(1,1e5)),ylim=c(-1,15),
     main="growth of largest DHf3 |Z| peaks \n log(|Z|*7/7.2077509) vs (sqrt(log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)))-1)/0.5 plot",xlab="(sqrt(log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)))-1)/0.5 value",ylab="log(|Z|*7/7.2077509) value");grid(col="gray2")
points((log(as.numeric(df2$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(dft$t)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points((log(sqrt(dfr$t/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(dgr$V1)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points((log(as.numeric(dhr$Q1))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points((log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=(log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=(log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points((log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points((log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points((log(as.numeric(dfDHf3$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(dfDHf3$Z*7/7.2077509),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)
points((log(as.numeric(dfDHf3_LLL$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(dfDHf3_LLL$Z*7/7.2077509),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)

abline(a=1.274014,b=.866363,col="brown",lty=6)


plot(log(as.numeric(df$V1)),log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=c(log(1e0),log(1e40)),ylim=log(c(0.5,100000)),
     main="growth of largest DHf4 |Z| peaks \n standard log(|Z|) vs log(t) plot",xlab="log(t) value",ylab="log(|Z|) value");grid(col="gray2")
points(log(as.numeric(df2$V1)),log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points(log(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)),log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points(log(as.numeric(dft$t)),log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points(log(dfr$t),log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(as.numeric(dgr$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(as.numeric(dhr$T)),log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(log(as.numeric(di$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(di$V1)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(di$V1)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf4$V1)),log(dfDHf4$Z),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)
points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf4_LLL$V1)),log(dfDHf4_LLL$Z),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)


plot(log(as.numeric(df$V2)),log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=c(log(1e0),log(1e25)),ylim=log(c(0.5,100000)),
     main="growth of largest DHf4 |Z| peaks \n log(|Z|*7/4.3042576) vs log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)) plot",xlab="log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)) value",ylab="log(|Z|*7/4.3042576) value");grid(col="gray2")
points(log(as.numeric(df2$V2)),log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(dft$t)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points(log(sqrt(dfr$t/2/pi)),log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(dgr$V1)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(as.numeric(dhr$Q1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)


points(log(as.numeric(di$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(di$V2)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(di$V2)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf4$V2)),log(dfDHf4$Z*7/4.3042576),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)
points(log(as.numeric(dfDHf4_LLL$V2)),log(dfDHf4_LLL$Z*7/4.3042576),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)

lines(seq(.1,50,by=.1),1.274014+0.866363*(seq(.1,50,by=.1)^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col="brown",lty=6)


plot((log(as.numeric(df$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=log(c(1,1e5)),ylim=c(-1,15),
     main="growth of largest DHf4 |Z| peaks \n log(|Z|*7/4.3042576) vs (sqrt(log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)))-1)/0.5 plot",xlab="(sqrt(log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^1*7)))-1)/0.5 value",ylab="log(|Z|*7/4.3042576) value");grid(col="gray2")
points((log(as.numeric(df2$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(dft$t)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points((log(sqrt(dfr$t/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(dgr$V1)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points((log(as.numeric(dhr$Q1))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points((log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=(log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=(log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points((log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points((log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)


points((log(as.numeric(dfDHf4$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(dfDHf4$Z*7/4.3042576),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)
points((log(as.numeric(dfDHf4_LLL$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(dfDHf4_LLL$Z*7/4.3042576),col=rgb(0,0,0,0.5),pch=20,cex=0.5)

abline(a=1.274014,b=.866363,col="brown",lty=6)


```
\elandscape

\clearpage

\blandscape
```{r different_rescalings_pt7, echo=FALSE, warnings=FALSE, fig.show = "hold",  out.width = c("32%","32%","32%"), fig.align = "center", fig.cap="A graphical comparison for the 2nd degree Ramanujan's Tau L-function and the Riemann Zeta function extreme peaks on the critical line of \\textbf{left panel} - $\\log\\left(|Z_L|\\right) \\text{ vs } \\log(t)$, \\textbf{middle panel} - $\\log\\left(|Z_L|\\cdot \\frac{N_C}{N_{\\chi\\ne0}}\\right) \\text{ vs } \\frac{1}{d}\\cdot\\log\\left(\\sqrt{(\\frac{t}{2\\pi})^d}\\right)+\\log\\left(\\sqrt{N_C}\\right)$, \\textbf{right panel} - $\\log\\left(|Z_L|\\cdot \\frac{N_C}{N_{\\chi\\ne0}}\\right) \\text{ vs } \\frac{\\left(\\sqrt{\\frac{1}{d}\\cdot\\log\\left(\\sqrt{(\\frac{t}{2\\pi})^d}\\right)+\\log\\left(\\sqrt{N_C}\\right)}-1\\right)}{0.5}$. The x axis rescaling in the middle and right panel is different in figure 10 compared to figures 4-9 because the two L-functions being compared in figure 10 are of different degrees.", fig.height=7}


plot(log(as.numeric(df$V1)),log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=c(log(1e0),log(1e40)),ylim=log(c(0.5,100000)),
     main="growth of largest Ramanujan Tau L-function |Z| peaks \n standard log(|Z|) vs log(t) plot",xlab="log(t) value",ylab="log(|Z|) value");grid(col="gray2")
points(log(as.numeric(df2$V1)),log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points(log(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)),log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points(log(as.numeric(dft$t)),log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points(log(dfr$t),log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(as.numeric(dgr$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(as.numeric(dhr$T)),log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(log(as.numeric(di$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(di$V1)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(di$V1)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(log(as.numeric(dfRaman$V1)),log(dfRaman$Z),col="magenta",pch=20,cex=0.5)


plot(log(as.numeric(df$V2)),log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=c(log(1e0),log(1e25)),ylim=log(c(0.5,100000)),
     main="growth of largest Ramanujan Tau L-function |Z| peaks \n log(|Z|*1/1) vs 1/2*log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^2*1))+log(sqrt(1)) plot",xlab="log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^2*1)) value",ylab="log(|Z|*1/1) value");grid(col="gray2")
points(log(as.numeric(df2$V2)),log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(dft$t)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points(log(sqrt(dfr$t/2/pi)),log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(sqrt(as.numeric(dgr$V1)/2/pi)),log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points(log(as.numeric(dhr$Q1)),log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)


points(log(as.numeric(di$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(di$V2)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(di$V2)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2)),log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points(1/2*log(as.numeric(dfRaman$V2)),log(dfRaman$Z),col="magenta",pch=20,cex=0.5)

lines(seq(.1,50,by=.1),1.274014+0.866363*(seq(.1,50,by=.1)^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col="brown",lty=6)


plot((log(as.numeric(df$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(df$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5,
     xlim=log(c(1,1e5)),ylim=c(-1,15),
     main="growth of largest Ramanujan Tau L-function |Z| peaks \n log(|Z|*1/1) vs (sqrt(1/2*log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^2*1))+log(sqrt(1)))-1)/0.5 plot",xlab="(sqrt(log(sqrt((t/2/pi)^2*1)))-1)/0.5 value",ylab="log(|Z|*1/1) value");grid(col="gray2")
points((log(as.numeric(df2$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(df2$Z),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.5)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$t)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(df_kotnick_nodup$Z))),pch=6,col="gray2",cex=0.3)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(dft$t)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dft$Z))),pch=6,col="gray",cex=0.3)

points((log(sqrt(dfr$t/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(dfr$Z)),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points((log(sqrt(as.numeric(dgr$V1)/2/pi))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dgr$V3))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)
points((log(as.numeric(dhr$Q1))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dhr$Z))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)

points((log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),pch=6,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=(log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))),x1=(log(as.numeric(di$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(di$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)

points((log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),pch=6,cex=0.5)
arrows(x0=(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,y0=log(0.9*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))),x1=(log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,col=rgb(0,1,1,0.2),
       y1=log(1.1*abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V4))), length=0.02, angle=90, code=3)
points((log(as.numeric(dilowerT$V2))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(abs(as.numeric(dilowerT$V5))),pch=20,col="gray",cex=0.4)


points(((1/2*log(as.numeric(dfRaman$V2)))^alpha_val-1)/alpha_val,log(dfRaman$Z),col="magenta",pch=20,cex=0.5)

abline(a=1.274014,b=.866363,col="brown",lty=6)


 

```
\elandscape

\clearpage


##Conclusions

A method for re-scaling the growth behaviour of extreme Riemann Siegel Z function of 1st degree L-functions with non-zero dirichlet characters of magnitude unity $|\chi|=1$ is demonstrated which allows easier comparison to the Riemann Zeta extreme Riemann Siegel Z function growth behaviour. Likewise an adjustment to the LLL algorithm based on the fractional exponents of the roots of unity associated with such L-functions is used to identify candidate locations for their extreme Riemann Siegel Z function peaks.

Using a transformation of the t co-ordinate based on the first quiescent region, a promising trend growth formula for extreme Riemann Siegel Z function peaks of the Riemann Zeta function is fitted to known montonically increasing |Z| peaks in the range 0 < t < 1e31. 


##Supplementary Material

A zip file of the located (extreme or otherwise) peak locations for the investigated L- functions and Davenport_Heilbronn counterparts is available on github https://github.com/johnpdmartin/sampling-investigations/blob/master/files_with_extreme_peaks_of_L_functions.zip. 


The typical format is 4-7 columns for files containing calculations by this author: 

column 1 - t value (accurate to 0.05), 

column 2 - N_1 value, 

column 3 - residual of N_1 value (used for investigating multi-modal distribution of peaks within [N,N+1) piecewise intervals. The file largest_value_zeta_insqrtsegment_0to3636_consolidatedfile.csv contains the extreme peak location in the lowest 3637 piecewise intervals of Riemann Zeta function.)

column 4 - |Z| value,

column 5 - Z value (sometimes calculated by more accurate method as crosscheck)

column 6 - text message describing method used for column 4 and 5

column 7 - may contain a number indicating element number of vector of estimates where peak occurred.

For files containing data from references there are typically column headers.



\clearpage


##References

1. Brent, R.P. (1979) "On the zeros of the Riemann zeta function in the critical strip" Math. Comp. 33 , p1361-1372
DOI: https://doi.org/10.1090/S0025-5718-1979-0537983-2

2. Brent R.P., van de Lune J., te Riele H.J.J. and Winter D.T. (1982) "On the Zeros of the Riemann Zeta function in the Critical Strip. II" Mathematics of Computation, Volume 39, Number 160, October 1982, Pages 681-688

3. van de Lune J. and te Riele H.J.J. "On the Zeros of the Riemann Zeta Function in the Critical Strip." III (1983) MATHEMATICS OF COMPUTATION VOLUME 41, NUMBER 164 OCTOBER PAGES 759-767

4. van de Lune J., te Riele H.J.J. and Winter D.T. (1986) “On the Zeros of the Riemann Zeta function in the Critical Strip. IV” Mathematics of Computation, Volume 46, Pages 667-681

5. A.M. Odlyzko, A. Schönhage, Fast algorithms for multiple evaluations of the Riemann zeta function, Trans. Am. Math. Soc., 309, (1988), 797–809.

6. Odlyzko, A. M. "On the Distribution of Spacings Between Zeros of the Zeta Function", (1987) Mathematics of Computation Vol 48, no. 177, 1987, pages 273-308 

7. Odlyzko, A.M. "The $10^{20}$-th zero of the Riemann Zeta function and 175 million of its neighbors". (1988) http://www.dtc.umn.edu/~odlyzko/unpublished/

8. Odlyzko A.M. "The $10^{22}$-nd zero of the Riemann zeta function, Dynamical, Spectral, and
Arithmetic Zeta Functions"  (2001) Amer. Math. Soc., Contemporary Math. series, no. 290,
139-144.

9. Gourdon X. and Sebah P., Numbers, Constants and Computation, http://numbers.computation.free.fr/Constants/constants.html, (1999) 
table of maximum Riemann Zeta peaks http://numbers.computation.free.fr/Constants/Miscellaneous/MaxiZAll.txt

10. Gourdon X. "The $10^{13}$-th first zeros of the Riemann Zeta function and zeros computation at very large height" (2004)
http://numbers.computation.free.fr/Constants/Miscellaneous/zetazeros1e13-1e24.pdf

11. Kotnick T., "COMPUTATIONAL ESTIMATION OF THE ORDER OF $\zeta$(1/2+it)" (2003) MATHEMATICS OF COMPUTATION, Volume 73, Number 246, Pages 949–956 S 0025-5718(03)01568-0

12. Hiary G.A., "Fast methods to compute the Riemann Zeta function" (2011)	
Annals of Mathematics, Valome 174, Pages 891-946	https://doi.org/http://dx.doi.org/10.4007/annals.2011.174.2.4

13. Hiary G.A., Table of high Riemann Zeta peaks (second table on the url page) https://people.math.osu.edu/hiary.1/fastmethods.html

14. Bober J.W. and Hiary G.A., "New computations of the Riemann zeta function on the critical line" (2018) Experimental Mathematics, 27(2), 125-137. https://doi.org/10.1080/10586458.2016.1233083

15. Tihanyi N., Kovács A. and Szűcs A. (2014) "Distributed computing of simultaneous Diophantine approximation problems" (2014) Stud. Univ. Babeş-Bolyai Math. 59, No. 4, 557–566

16. Tihanyi N., Kovacs A. and Kovacs J., "Computing Extremely Large Values of the Riemann Zeta Function" (2017) J Grid Computing 15:527–534
https://doi.org/10.1007/s10723-017-9416-0

17. Tihanyi N. and Kovacs A. "IMPROVEMENTS ON FINDING LARGE VALUES OF THE RIEMANN ZETA FUNCTION ON THE CRITICAL LINE" (2018) Annales Univ. Sci. Budapest., Sect. Comp., 48, p53–64

18. Tihanyi N. "Numerical computing of extremely large values of the Riemann-Siegel Z-function" (2019) Doctoral Thesis, Eötvös Loránd University

19. Martin, J.P.D. "Preliminary estimates of extreme Riemann Zeta function peak heights with $\pm 10\%$ accuracy on the critical line using spectrally filtered heavily truncated partial Euler Product calculations" (2026) 
http://dx.doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.31990179

20. Martin, J.P.D. "Preliminary estimates of extreme Riemann Zeta function peak heights on three lines $s=\{0+I\cdot t, 1+I\cdot t, 1.5+I\cdot t\}$ away from the real axis with $\pm 1\%, \pm 1\%, \pm 0.1\%$ accuracy for $10^{12} < t < 10^{40}$ using spectrally filtered heavily truncated partial Euler Product calculations." (2026)
http://dx.doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.32129782

21. Riemann, Bernhard (1859). "$\ddot{U}$ber die Anzahl der Primzahlen unter einer gegebenen Gr$\ddot{o}$sse". Monatsberichte der Berliner Akademie.. In Gesammelte Werke, Teubner, Leipzig (1892), Reprinted by Dover, New York (1953).

22. Titchmarsh, E.C. (1986) The Theory of the Riemann Zeta Function. 2nd Revised (Heath-Brown, D.R. ) Edition, Oxford University Press, Oxford.

23. Edwards, H.M. (1974). Riemann’s zeta function. Pure and Applied Mathematics 58. New York-London:
Academic Press. ISBN 0-12-232750-0. Zbl 0315.10035.

24. Berry, M. V. “The Riemann-Siegel Expansion for the Zeta Function: High Orders and Remainders.”
Proc. Roy. Soc. London A 450, 439-462, 1995.

25. J. Arias De Reyna, “High precision computation of Riemann’s Zeta function by the Riemann-Siegel formula”, Mathematics of Computation Vol 80, no. 274, 2011, Pages 995–1009

26. The LMFDB Collaboration, The L-functions and Modular Forms Database, \url{http://www.lmfdb.org}, 2019, [Online; accessed January 2020].

27. Spira, R. Mathematics of Computation, Volume 63, Number 208,
October 1994, Pages 747-748

28. Balanzario, E.P. and Sanchez-Ortiz, J. Mathematics of Computation,
Volume 76, Number 260, October 2007, Pages 2045–2049

29. E. Bombieri, A. Ghosh, “Around the Davenport–Heilbronn function”, Uspekhi Mat. Nauk, 66:2(398) (2011), 15–66; Russian Math. Surveys, 66:2 (2011), 221–270
https://doi.org/10.4213/rm9410 
IAS lecture https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-JUHypc2_9A

30. Martin, J.P.D. "7-, \& 9-periodic dual Davenport Heilbronn counterexamples are derived for the corresponding Degree 1 dual L-functions pairs given in https://www.lmfdb.org/L/degree1." (2020)
http://dx.doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.11675877

31.  Keiper, J. B. "On the Zeros of the Ramanujan $tau$-Dirichlet Series in the Critical Strip." Mathematics of Computation 65, no. 216 (1996): 1613-619. Accessed February 10, 2021. http://www.jstor.org/stable/2153727.

32. Lenstra A.K., Lenstra Jr. H.W., Lovász L., "Factoring polynomials with rational coefficients" (1982) Math. Ann., 261, (4), 515–534.

33. The PARI~Group, PARI/GP version 2.12.0, Univ. Bordeaux, 2018,
\url{http://pari.math.u-bordeaux.fr/}.

34. R Core Team (2017). R: A language and environment for statistical computing.
R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria. \url{https://www.R-project.org/}.

35. RStudio Team (2015). RStudio: Integrated Development for R. RStudio, Inc., Boston, MA \url{http://www.rstudio.com/}.

36. Box, G. E. P., & Cox, D. R. (1964). An analysis of transformations. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B (Methodological), 26(2), 211–252. 
https://www.jstor.org/stable/2984418

37. Venables, W. N. & Ripley, B. D. (2002) Modern Applied Statistics with S. Fourth Edition. Springer, New York. ISBN 0-387-95457-0
